channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: What to know about the Meta glasses the New Orleans attacker used to scout the French Quarter
item.description: The man who drove a truck into a crowd of people in New Orleans on New Year’s Day, killing 14, had previously scouted the French Quarter and recorded video with his Meta smart glasses, the FBI said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Fubo combining with Disney's Hulu + Live TV; lawsuit against Venu Sports settled
item.description: Disney's Hulu + Live TV and sports streaming service Fubo are combining in a deal that will also see the settlement of a lawsuit against the creation of Venu Sports.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Hacked Chrome extensions put 2.6 million users at risk of data leak
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says hackers are embedding dangerous code in Chrome extensions to steal your data.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Your iPhone could soon measure your stress levels, thanks to this hormone-testing stick
item.description: The Eli Health Hormometer aims to gauge stress levels, reproductive health and more by analyzing your saliva.
item.dc:creator: alexblake.techradar@gmail.com (Alex Blake), TechRadar

item.title: Top tech trends powering the Middle East’s digital evolution by 2025
item.description: Q. Looking ahead to 2025, what do you see as the key technology trends that will shape the Middle East’s digital landscape? A. By 2025, several key technology trends will shape the Middle East’s digital landscape.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Dating Sunday 2025: The busiest day on dating apps is almost here
item.description: Dating apps are gearing up for their busiest day of the year: Dating Sunday. This landmark day in the dating world always lands on the first Sunday of January.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Elon Musk Defends Tesla Cybertruck After Explosion in Las Vegas - NewsNation
item.description: A Tesla Cybertruck exploded outside of the Trump International Hotel in Las Vegas on New Year's Day, injuring seven people and killing its driver Matthew Livelsberger. Elon Musk defended the vehicle on social media, stating that it contained the explosion and directed the blast upwards.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: The step I take to clear my inbox every January
item.description: It's easy to find yourself swamped in unread emails. Here are a few simple steps to archiving them and clearing out some of your digital clutter.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What to know about Apple's $95 million settlement of the snooping Siri case
item.description: Apple has agreed to pay $95 million to settle a lawsuit that accused the company of turning its virtual assistant Siri into a snoop that eavesdropped on the users of iPhones and other trendy devices in a betrayal to its long-standing commitment to personal privacy.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Chinese cyber firm linked to botnet attack sanctioned in US
item.description: The federal government hit Chinese cyber group Integrity Technology Group with new sanctions on Friday, citing alleged computer intrusion incidents against those in the U.S. At the direction of the China, hackers associated with the Integrity Technology Group allegedly targeted multiple U.S.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Appeals court blocks return of US net neutrality rules for ISPs
item.description: The reintroduction of neutrality rules for network providers is looking increasingly unlikely, at least for the next five years, after a US appeals court blocked efforts by the US Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to reinstate them.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Apple settles Siri eavesdropping accusations for $95M
item.description: Apple has agreed to pay $95 million to settle a class-action lawsuit alleging the tech giant used its voice assistant Siri to obtain and share private conversations without users' permission.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Californians, beware of this FasTrak toll fee text scam
item.description: Avoid falling for FasTrak toll text scams. If you receive one of these messages, do not click on any links and do not provide any personal information.
item.dc:creator: Ryanne Mena, The Mercury News

item.title: China rolls out its crime-fighting ball to chase down criminals
item.description: A Chinese company has developed a spherical, 276-pound law enforcement robot that has been chasing down criminals at impressive speeds.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Apple to pay $95 million to settle lawsuit accusing Siri of eavesdropping
item.description: Apple has agreed to pay $95 million to settle a lawsuit accusing the privacy-minded company of deploying its virtual assistant Siri to eavesdrop on people using its iPhone and other trendy devices.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: No surprise: AI, cyber continued to dominate in 2024
item.description: Current and former federal technology executives weighed in on what topics drove the storylines in 2024. The post No surprise: AI, cyber continued to dominate in 2024 first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Health AI: How Apple can boost public health
item.description: Combined with its existing solutions, Apple’s strategic approach to artificial intelligence (AI) deployment could make a radical difference to public health. Here is how it could achieve that.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: IRS deploys AI tools to combat emerging tech’s role in new fraud schemes
item.description: AI tools are accelerating the development of deepfake schemes and disinformation campaigns that led to fraud The IRS is trying to keep up. The post IRS deploys AI tools to combat emerging tech’s role in new fraud schemes first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jory Heckman, Federal News Network

item.title: Agencies required to share custom software under new law
item.description: The SHARE IT Act, now law, builds on previous efforts to ensure agencies are sharing common software code across government. The post Agencies required to share custom software under new law first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Rogue Two
item.description: Don Parente, the vice president of sales and solution architecture for MetTel Federal, provides recommendations for agencies to better manage space IT. The post Rogue Two — Your IT sprawl is about to expand to space first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Revealed: The 10 most popular and worst passwords of 2024
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson cites a new report that says many people still use weak passwords like "123456," risking their accounts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Venezuela's top court issues a $10 million fine for TikTok over allegedly deadly video challenges
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Venezuela’s Supreme Court on Monday issued a $10 million fine against TikTok for “not implementing measures” to prevent viral video challenges that have allegedly led to the deaths of three Venezuelan children recently.
item.dc:creator: JUAN PABLO ARRAEZ and MANUEL RUEDA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Rhode Islanders' data was leaked from a cyberattack on state health benefits website
item.description: PROVIDENCE, R.I. (AP) — Cybercriminals who hacked Rhode Island’s system for health and benefits programs have released files to a site on the dark web, a scenario the state has been preparing for, Gov. Daniel McKee said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Homeland Security helps write the book on how to acquire artificial intelligence
item.description: Few departments have more diverse procurement needs than Homeland Security. The agency operates on natural disasters, immigration activity and cybersecurity. The post Homeland Security helps write the book on how to acquire artificial intelligence first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network
